SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper clearly states its main claims and supports them with quantitative NLL results, human AB preference tests, and targeted experiments (Sections 2–3, e.g., Table 3 and Section 3.2). Mathematical formulation of the model is straightforward and appears correct (Eqs. 1–9) and the use of TBPTT and hierarchical modules is well motivated. However, some methodological weaknesses remain: the WaveNet baseline is a re-implementation with admitted differences (Section 3.1), and the human evaluation and NLL comparisons do not report statistical significance or confidence intervals. These issues are minor and do not invalidate the main empirical conclusions but do reduce the rigor of some comparisons.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The multi-timescale hierarchical use of RNNs conditioning a sample-level autoregressive MLP for raw waveform generation is a novel and non-trivial architecture compared to prior work (Sections 1–2 and Appendix A). The paper positions itself relative to WaveNet and multi-scale RNN literature (Related Work) and demonstrates a distinct design choice (stateful RNN tiers + discrete sample softmax). While it builds on existing ideas (dilated convolutions, multi-scale RNNs, and autoregressive modeling), the particular combination and focus on end-to-end raw audio generation represent a notable and clearly differentiated contribution.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is organized in the standard logical flow (introduction, model, experiments, related work, conclusion) and clearly states contributions up front (end of Section 1). The model description includes equations (Section 2) and implementation/training details (Section 3), making reproduction feasible in large part. Minor clarity issues include acknowledged missing implementation details for the WaveNet baseline (Section 3.1) and several references to figures/tables (e.g., Fig. 1, Table 1) that are not shown in the provided text; also some duplicated/garbled sentences appear in the supplied document, which slightly impairs readability.
